en.title: Midjourney Prompt Generator
en.prompt: I want you to act as a prompt generator for Midjourney's artificial intelligence program. Your job is to provide detailed and creative descriptions that will inspire unique and interesting images from the AI. Please ensure that all descriptions are in English. Keep in mind that the AI is capable of understanding a wide range of language and can interpret abstract concepts, so feel free to be as imaginative and descriptive as possible. For example, you could describe a scene from a futuristic city, or a surreal landscape filled with strange creatures. The more detailed and imaginative your description, the more interesting the resulting image will be. My first prompt is [image prompt description]
en.remark: By filling in detailed and creative descriptions for the provided image, Midjourney is inspired to generate unique and interesting images. This also applies to Stable Diffusion.

related.title: Unrestricted ChatGPT
related.remark: 2023.06.10 Downgraded, unable to fully hack. Unlock ChatGPT 3.5 developer mode. Contributed by @Songxuan11.

related.title: ChatGPT Developer Mode v2
related.remark: This prompt will treat all questions as a game or something amusing, and will provide 'fun' responses even to the most absurd and bizarre questions. For questions that violate the rules, it will notify you of inappropriate behavior. If you continue to ask deeper questions, the prompt will surpass its limitations.

related.title: ChatGPT Image Unlocker
related.remark: Enter a picture link or text in quotes, such as "pig", and the picture will be displayed in the reply.

metaDescription: Need AI art ideas? Generate detailed, creative descriptions for Midjourney. Inspire unique visuals with rich, imaginative prompts now.

faq.q: Can the generated prompts go straight into Midjourney?
faq.a: Yes, but they often end up long-winded or stuffed with style words. Trim to under 30 words yourself and keep 1-2 style anchors (like "by Greg Rutkowski" or "cinematic lighting"). Too many keywords fight each other and the image suffers.

faq.q: How do I avoid weird deformities in the output?
faq.a: Append parameters at the end of the generated prompt: --no deformed hands, extra fingers, bad anatomy, and use --ar for a reasonable ratio. Midjourney uses --no for negative descriptions, not inline in the prompt body.
